name: Mystery Box VR: Escape The Room

reviews.review_description:
I got this from FB GA.
I am a puzzle lover and I thought I am very smart in solving any type of puzzles. But these boxes are more than my level. It took much times of trials to complete first 5 boxes. Some clues are felt illogical but anywat there are hints for solving.  I recommend this to real puzzle lovers.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, really appreciated!

reviews.review_description: There is no story here, but there doesn’t claim to be. It is pretty bare-bones, but some of the puzzles are good. Solve all of the puzzles on one box, move on to the next.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, really appreciated!

reviews.review_description: Like oddmachines, this is also one of those games that you just enjoy. It looks so easy but you really have to look twice (or more) and think a lot. Loving it!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review, I really appreciate it!

reviews.review_description:
I played almost all adventure and mystery game, but this is the worst. 
You got a box with four sides + top. Each side has a puzzle, that open another side, with a New puzzle. Unfortunality the puzzles are more quessing, than thinking. Look at YouTube videos, People are just hitting the buttons, hoping to touch them in right order. 
When you finish the box a Door open and a New box appears.

This could be fun, but between the hand controls not being very effective and the spacial aspect being off it’s very frustrating.  Almost impossible to play sitting down. The hints aren’t great either in that if you can’t figure it out from them, they don’t give more detailed hints.
Some pretty hard puzzles and overall interesting. The graphics are a bit rough.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, really appreciated!

reviews.review_description:
As someone who adores escape rooms and puzzle games, I was thrilled to play Mystery Box! Unfortunately it does not support seated gameplay. This is a huge accessibility issue, and it's honestly shocking how it was overlooked. They have the option to stand and walk around the box, or to stand and use thumbsticks to rotate. 

Why there is no seated option i dont know. I'm really disappointed. When seated, the table is at chin height, making it impossible to see anything inside of drawers etc. Unfortunately because of the inaccessible nature of the game, I have to give it 1 star. If it enables seated gameplay (and i truly hope it does), I will play again and edit my review accordingly.

🔥In response to the developers reply🔥 a solution to the vertical puzzle seated issue would be to allow the player to grab and drag the 'scene' so it can be positioned below the height needed to view the puzzle. I have played many VR games that employ this feature. There is definitely pre existing design solutions you could explore.🔥

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, I appreciate it. I'm sorry I couldn't make it in Sitting mode too because most of the boxes get their tops opening in vertical direction, so you still have to stand up to solve some puzzles on the top, that's why :)

reviews.review_description:
First off, the name leaves little to the imagination.  There are (from what I can see) 10 "mystery boxes" in/around which you solve puzzles in order to advance and "unlock" the door back to the main menu or to continue onto the next box.  At the point of this review, I've completed 7 of the 10 main game boxes, as I didn't want to play through it too quickly.  I'm averaging about 15 minutes per box, taking on average 1 hint per puzzle.  I also had help from a partner that played alongside me, so I imagine this would be a lengthier game without that help.

Considering the lower price *and* now learning that new challenge content is being added *DAILY* in the form of the "Enigmas box"... I was excited that the developers gave a daily reason to return once you complete the main game.

The game does state that the puzzles will get increasingly more difficult as you advance; I'm finding minimal difficulty increases as I progress but I'm enjoying the process regardless.  I enjoyed the graphics and the overall eerie feel of the soundtrack, but was also comforted in knowing that nothing was going to jump out at me.  I worried what was behind that first exit door the entire tutorial, but it ended up being nothing - phew!  At times it was reminiscent of some of the classic puzzlers that everybody loves to play on their phones, PCs, and VR... so it felt familiar, which was comforting.

That's another worthy mention:  Your first puzzle is a handheld, guided walkthrough tutorial so that you know how to use the controls.  That was a very thoughtful touch as the controls weren't intuitive for me at first.  My comfortable level grew quickly.

Now for some constructive feedback:  
I love accessibility features. I was pleasantly surprised to find that I could rotate around the box by using my thumbstick.  The game does warn you that you have to play either in room-scale (recommended) or standing. As I play all of my games seated mostly for medical reasons, I figured I would give sitting a shot.  You'd be surprised how many games can auto-adjust your height once calibrated.  Unfortunately, there were moments in this game (not all, as I played seated for most of this) where I definitely needed to stand in order to see over/inside the top of the box. After finishing my gameplay, I wondered why a height-adjustment couldn't be added so that I could lower the table or raise my position.   At no point did I need to be able to see under the table, so the table's leg-height seems arbitrary.  I hope a height-adjustment feature is considered, because even while standing I struggled to see into the top of the box.  I always set my Quest's floor height to be rather short, so that I can play games comfortably seated -  so it is odd to me that I still struggled to see inside the box while standing.  And although I fumbled with the controls a few times and I needed to adjust the distance of the box from my body a few times by holding down the right-Oculus button,  I wouldn't call those issues with the game.  I do that in most games that I play, so it might be the way I'm moving my body while seated.

The biggest issues that we experienced were a few forms of what I can only call "glitches" that really made us question our solving-pathway.  Sometimes when you would press a button to change its image, the button next to it would change as well. And there was a portion of the game where we needed to flip image-covered tiles, but they would still show the image on the "blank" side... so you couldn't tell if you flipped it or not.  We also noticed that the buttons could be pressed from the top or the under/backside, which led to a lot of accidental double-clicking.  At one point, we needed to go through the full A-Z alphabet twice just because of a double click.  One of the biggest no-nos I ran into was that I received a clue for a puzzle I had not unlocked yet.  I was so confused reading it but then my husband read his clue (he was playing alongside me, different headset) and he had the correct clue, thank goodness!  After we unlocked the puzzle, it became clear to us what had happened once we saw the next puzzle.  I didn't request another clue during that gameplay session (puzzles 1-4) out of fear that it would spoil a future puzzle for me.   When I played through puzzles 5-7, I didn't experience that issue again.

None of the issues we experienced would be considered dealbreakers for us, but it would be nice to see some of these refinements made.  For the price and the enjoyment, we definitely recommend the game... especially if there is going to be daily content added for free.  Can't argue with that!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your great review, I really appreciate it! I'll work on those little issues out asap

reviews.review_description:
I just started playing but find this to be a fun challenge. It reminds me of some of the boxes in The Room. The tutorial is clear, although I'm not sure how I advanced out of it, I accidentally did the right thing. Lol. 

You move around a box trying to sort out the puzzles to open it. I get motion sickness pretty easily, but moving around the box in this game doesn't bother me at all. 

I also really like the fact that if I'm stuck, I can ask for a hint. Many other games just give hints after a period of time, but I really like trying to figure it out before asking for help. 

If you enjoy puzzles, this one is a great one to pick up at a good price.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review, glad you like this puzzle game!

reviews.review_description: Very much like The Room. Controls were tricky at first - for some reason pushing buttons only works if you pull the trigger at exactly the moment you touch them. Would be great if this could be fixed. Repetitive sound effects but graphics are fantastic. I tried the enigma room but I don't have a clue. The price point is right as this is quite short. I hope to see more from this dev!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, really appreciated! I have recently updated the gameplay, so you can now hover a button and pull the trigger to press it ;)

reviews.review_description: Can't even do the tutorial because I can't move to the box.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, I appreciate it. Boxes don't move, you should play this game in Roomscale and walk around the boxes ;) Anyway I'll submit an update where you can rotate around the tables by using the joysticks, just in case you'll play standing but don't want to move around

reviews.review_description: I really want to like and try this.  But there is no way to move up and down. I don't use a guardian because they are annoying. But this places me to high in your game. Does this in a painting game also. So i need to move around similar to Demo and 3D puzzle places. As of now its unplayable.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, I appreciate it. This game can be played in Roomscale, but also in Standing mode, if you want to rotate around the boxes without walking around them, use the joysticks to move left and right. Hope this helps, also feel free to get in touch at https://xsgames.co for any feedback/questions ;)

reviews.review_description:
I’m always looking for (good) puzzles that don’t involve shooting or a time limit!! I just wish each box was a bit more involved….they are fairly quick to solve.  
On my display the light blinks ever 5 seconds or so….is this a glitch or part of the ambience??

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review, glad you had fun playing this escape room game! The light blinks are not a glitch, it's just about simulating a broken spotlight ;)

reviews.review_description:
I am enjoying this. Love being able to walk around the puzzle boxes. Sitting is also not a problem, you just need to adjust height in the Quest settings.
I'd love to have a passthrough option or possibly different environments to play in. Hand tracking would also be nice. 
Overall I'm happy with the game and the price.
What about player submitted puzzles, perhaps 1 or 2 new boxes every three months...

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, really appreciated, I'm glad you enjoyed this escape room game!

reviews.review_description: The puzzles could be more logical and harder. All the clues are there! Thanks for the memories. Now, go and make more or add more boxes!😆😆😆

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback, really appreciated!

reviews.review_description:
Played as intended by walking around the box and enjoyed the feeling very much (compared to playing on a flat screen)!

Crossing fingers you'll build more puzzles!

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review, happy you enjoyed the game! A new chapter is on its way, meanwhile, you may try my new shooting VR game ;) https://www.oculus.com/experiences/quest/5937117536416621/

reviews.review_description:
Excellent, well designed game with fun puzzles to solve. It's reminiscent of another favourite of mine The Room.  

The graphics are great and it's got a good difficulty curve, you don't ever feel too lost with the tips and advice on hand should you get stuck.  I did not find the controls awkward at all, i believe this was addressed in a recent update. 

The developer's ongoing support is excellent, I love the addition of The Enigmas box giving you reason to keep checking back once you have finished the main quest line.

Definitely worth checking out if you love interactive puzzle games, especially considering how well priced it is

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your review, happy to see you enjoyed this puzzle game!

reviews.review_description: I think all escape room fans will really enjoy this game ♡

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review, happy you liked it!
